summary: Addresses two external review comments from greptile-apps[bot]: (1) adds DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive to python-base apt-get RUN step to prevent potential interactive tzdata prompts in CI, (2) corrects stale documentation in container-images.md to accurately describe the constraint file path as releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt. Both fixes are minimal, correctly scoped, and match the suggested changes verbatim. Two new review pattern files document the lessons learned.

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
No scope creep beyond reviewer feedback

metadata.title: C001: Add Keystone container image and release config

review_details.step_1_intent: Address two external review comments from greptile-apps[bot]: fix DEBIAN_FRONTEND for tzdata and correct stale doc path.
review_details.step_2_scope: Changes confined to images/python-base/Dockerfile (1 line) and docs/reference/container-images.md (3 lines). Metadata files (.planwerk/) updated for tracking. No scope creep.
review_details.step_3_verification: Dockerfile syntax valid. No automated tests affected — Dockerfiles are verified via docker build (task 3.1, already completed). Shell tests for apply-constraint-overrides.sh are unaffected by these changes.
review_details.step_4_patterns: SPDX headers intact. DEBIAN_FRONTEND correctly applied only to the Dockerfile containing tzdata (python-base). Other Dockerfiles (venv-builder, keystone) install no interactive packages — no DEBIAN_FRONTEND needed. Consistent with the new review pattern.
review_details.step_5_tests: No test changes needed. The Dockerfile change is a build-time environment variable, not testable via shell tests. The doc change is prose only.
review_details.step_6_security: DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive is a security/reliability improvement — prevents non-deterministic CI builds.
review_details.step_7_complexity: Both fixes are single-line or few-line changes. Minimal possible solution.
review_details.step_8_completeness: Both external review comments fully addressed. Documentation now matches script implementation. Dockerfile now sets noninteractive frontend for apt.
